programme.title: Episode #5.10
programme.desc: The victim's name was Dale Harrell. He was married for ten years and was soon to be a partner at his company as a successful mechanical engineer. He made 83, 000 a year and provided a safe home for his wife and three children in Gilbert, Arizona. At approximately 2;36 AM on January 14, 2009, his wife of ten years, Marissa DeVault, put a hammer in his husband's head five times while he was fast asleep, only hours after having sex. Her first 911 calls to the Gilbert Police Department claimed abuse and rape. The ensuing investigation revealed a story known only to a few and a life kept secret from a husband. There was a roommate with a gun. There was a secret life of a millionaire lover. And, from her days as an exotic dancer, Marissa attempted to hire her former lover and bouncer of a strip club. The killer was not a visitor to the club and she was not a bartender or server. Instead, she could be found on the stage as an entertainer. How did all these characters come together to result in the death of Dale Harrell? Where did the gun come from and how was it instrumental in revealing the truths of a killer? Who was her "Sugar Daddy" and was he an accomplice? Why was the hammer used to kill instead of a gun when the gun was only mere feet of the woman accused. It is a story filled with twists, turns, characters and lust and, in the end, a victim lay dead. His name was Dale Harrell.

programme.desc: The team looks to cross The Great Pile.

programme.title: Thor: God of Thunder
programme.desc: Thor's Uncle Loki has escaped Asgard in search of Yggdrasil.

programme.title: Blitzkrieg
programme.desc: Running out of time, Scott, Lydia and Malia decide to storm the rift in a desperate attempt to get Stiles back. Liam, Hayden and Mason make a deal with Theo in order to learn Douglas's real plan.

programme.title: Memory Found
programme.desc: While Liam and Theo act as decoys, Scott, Lydia and Malia enact a desperate plan to try and remember Stiles.

programme.title: Riders On The Storm
programme.desc: With the fate of Beacon Hills hanging in the balance, Scott and his pack face off against Douglas in a final showdown.

programme.title: Said The Spider To The Fly
programme.desc: As Scott readies Beacon Hills for his imminent departure, a mysterious presence breaks out of Eichen House.

programme.title: Raw Talent
programme.desc: Scott and Malia search for the owner of a unique bullet, fearing that a new breed of werewolf hunter has come to Beacon Hills. At the same time, Lydia must face her own fears of Eichen House.

programme.title: After Images
programme.desc: While Scott and the others desperately pursue a missing werewolf, Melissa and Argent investigate the nature of a strange body.

programme.title: Face-To-Faceless
programme.desc: Scott, Lydia and Malia have to take drastic measures to quell the growing violence in Beacon Hills, while Liam endures his own gauntlet at the high school.

programme.title: Pressure Test
programme.desc: When Scott and the others try to protect two new werewolves, an unlikely ally joins the fight.

programme.title: Triggers
programme.desc: Liam and Theo attempt to draw the Hunters away from Beacon Hills.

programme.title: Werewolves Of London
programme.desc: After a shocking attack leaves the pack reeling, Scott sets out to recruit reinforcements.

programme.title: Genotype
programme.desc: Scott and the others must make a discovery before it’s too late.

programme.title: The Probe
programme.desc: When a cargo plane carrying five passengers is downed in the eye of a hurricane, the passengers find themselves trapped in an eerily cavernous chamber and dependent upon the "human" compassion of their alien captors for survival. Mark Richman stars.

programme.title: Sandkings (Parts One and Two)
programme.desc: Beau Bridges stars in this bone-chilling thriller. A ride of harrowing suspense, this taut film is packed with heart-stopping tension that faithfully fulfills the tradition of the ground-breaking series upon which it is based, the "Outer Limits."

programme.title: Valerie 23
programme.desc: Can jealousy be a good thing? Not for Rachel Rose, a physical therapist whose life is threatened when she come between her paraplegic patient and his amorous robotic companion who is programmed to remove any obstacle that stands in her way.

programme.title: Blood Brothers
programme.desc: Two brothers wrestle for control of their father's million-dollar pharmaceutical company. But when one brother discovers a drug which may hold the secret to eternal life, the battle for control becomes a rapid trip toward self-destruction.

programme.title: The Second Soul
programme.desc: A dying race of aliens come to Earth to stop its own extinction...by reanimating human corpses. But a government doctor (Mykelti Williamson) senses that there may be more to their plans than mere survival! D. W. Moffett and Rae Dawn Chong also star.

programme.title: White Light Fever
programme.desc: An experiment at St. Peter's Hospital threatens the natural order of things when an embittered billionaire (William Hickey) pays brilliant cardiologist (Bruce Davidson) to help him cheat death. But is death willing to be cheated so easily?

programme.title: The Choice
programme.desc: Aggie (Thora Birch) is an outcast at her elementary school because strange things seem to happen around her. When she is suspended from school, her parents hire a nanny who seems to good to be true. Will they lose their only child to keep her safe?

programme.title: Virtual Future
programme.desc: When a college student (Josh Brolin) experimenting with time travel takes job with a prestigious lab, he doesn't realize that the lab's billionaire owner understands completely the financial and political benefits of seeing into the future.

programme.title: Living Hell
programme.desc: Ben Kohler's (Sam Robards) life is saved after having an experimental microchip implanted in his brain, but when he starts seeing murders as if through the eyes of the killer, it becomes clear that something is desperately wrong.

programme.title: Archie In The Lock-Up
programme.desc: Mike and Lionel are planning on attending a demonstration at the United Nations. However, the rally gets out of hand and news of it appears on TV. This worries Gloria who begs Archie to go down there and get Mike. Later, Mike and Lionel return with reports that protesters were arrested. Pretty soon, the family figures out that Archie has been mistakenly arrested and is trapped in jail with a group of radicals.

programme.title: The Election Story
programme.desc: A local election has the Bunker family in turmoil as Mike and Gloria are all for Claire Packer, a woman, which is exactly why Archie is dead set against her. However, once down at the voting booths, Archie soon realizes that he can't vote because he isn't registered, leaving it up to him to try to convince Edith to vote for his candidate of choice.

programme.title: Edith's Accident
programme.desc: At the grocery store, Edith has an accident in the parking lot. She loses control of her shopping cart which smashes into a car and a can of Cling Peaches (in heavy syrup) flies out of the cart, onto the car, and makes a dent. She immediately leaves a note on the car with her name and number. This boggles Archie's mind and he does all he can to try to get out of paying for damages.

programme.title: Mike's Problem
programme.desc: Mike is all stressed out with upcoming exams leaving him impotent and frustrated. Gloria encourages him to discuss it with her, but he refuses. Gloria confides in Edith what the problem is, and in turn she tells Archie. This leads Archie to enter the situation, trying to give Mike advice, but this just makes matters worse.

programme.title: The Blockbuster
programme.desc: The Bunkers are having problems with their furnace which gets Archie entertaining the idea of possibly moving to California. When an unscrupulous black real-estate salesman, Mr. Byrd, offers Archie a hefty sum of money for his home, Archie is all for it despite Mike's warnings that Byrd is a blockbuster (someone who persuades white home-owners to sell by appealing to the fear that minority groups will move into the neighborhood causing property values to decline).

programme.title: The Insurance Is Cancelled
programme.desc: Archie comes home from work in a particularly awful mood because he has to make an important decision at work: whether to lay off a minority worker or a white man. His decision to lay off a Puerto Rican leads to an interesting visit to the Bunker household. To compound his stress, Archie learns that his insurance has been canceled when his neighborhood is redlined as a bad risk.

programme.title: Christmas Day At The Bunkers
programme.desc: It's Christmas Day and Archie is the only one not in the Christmas spirit. Mike and Gloria question him, but he refuses to reveal what's bugging him. The Jeffersons come over to wish the Bunkers a "Merry Christmas", but this does anything but cheer Archie up especially since Henry is dressed as Santa.

programme.title: The Man In The Street
programme.desc: Archie comes home from work in a great mood and orders Edith to have dinner ready and waiting, because he has an important date to watch the CBS Evening News to see himself on it. Apparently, Archie was interviewed as a man-on-the-street voicing his opinions on Nixon's presidency. However, getting to see himself proves to be difficult for him, when the TV breaks down.

programme.title: Cousin Maude's Visit
programme.desc: Edith has been running herself ragged caring for Archie, Mike and Gloria, who have all come down with a terrible flu. This prompts her to call on her extremely liberal Cousin Maude to come and stay with the Bunkers and help out. Maude proves to be salt in an already open wound for Archie, who has never liked Maude and vice versa.

programme.title: Edith's Problem
programme.desc: Archie, Mike and Gloria are stunned when Edith becomes a rollercoaster of moods, going from joy to anger to tears in a snap of a finger. Gloria sits her down and suggests that perhaps she's beginning menopause. This worries Edith, who becomes convinced that Archie doesn't love her and find her attractive anymore. Meanwhile, the family tries to cope with Edith.

programme.title: The Elevator Story
programme.desc: The Bunkers are celebrating Edith's birthday at an Italian restaurant in the city when Archie discovers that Edith failed to mail out a payment to the insurance company. This leads him to run across the street to drop it off. However, on his way he gets stuck in an elevator along with a pregnant Puerto Rican and her husband, an aging hippie, and an erudite black businessman.

programme.title: Archie And The Fbi
programme.desc: A man shows up at the Bunker's door claiming to be a government agent and begins asking questions about Larry Grundy, a neighbor of the Bunkers. Archie soon becomes paranoid when he receives a phone call from a neighbor saying that Archie too is being investigated. As paranoia overwhelms him, Archie becomes convinced that he's being watched that perhaps the house is bugged.

programme.title: Archie Sees A Mugging
programme.desc: Archie and Mike head down to the store to get some items and on the way out Archie witnesses a mugging. Archie doesn't want to get involved in the matter and refuses to tell the police what he saw. However, when he is forced to get involved, he embellishes his story, citing that the mugging was an attempted mafia hit.

programme.title: Mike's Mysterious Son
programme.desc: Edith answers the door to find a woman on the stoop with a young boy. The woman casually mentions that she's dropping Mike's son off and promptly leaves. Edith now finds herself in the awkward position of explaining to the rest of the family where the boy came from. When he finds out, a stunned Mike doesn't know what to say, but does admit to Gloria that he had been intimate with the boy's mother.

programme.title: Archie & Edith Alone
programme.desc: Mike and Gloria go away for eight days to spend some time in a commune, leaving Archie and Edith to spend some quality time together. Archie and Edith settle down for a quiet game of cards but when she lets him win, Archie insults her and they get into a big fight. Edith refuses to forgive Archie until he apologizes.

programme.title: Edith Gets A Mink
programme.desc: Edith gets a package in the mail and discovers that it is a mink cape sent to her as a 'thank you' gift by her Cousin Amelia for Edith taking care of her after a surgery. When Archie sees the cape, he is furious (since he takes it the wrong way) and demands that she give it back. However, that's when the cape is ruined in an accident and Archie sees a chance to make a three-hundred-dollar profit.

programme.title: Grown-Up Girly Girl
programme.desc: Clinton Kelly and Stacy London return to make fashion fantasies come true! They begin with Jessica, a petite young mom who has neglected self-care and still dresses like a teenager. With her kids growing up, Jessica seeks guidance to uncover her own style, aiming to embrace a sophisticated look that will reflect her confidence as a grown woman. It‚Äôs time for her to feel great in her own skin!

programme.title: Punk Rock God
programme.desc: Stacy and Clinton meet Patrick, a charming “brewery dad” eager to swap his t-shirts and clogs for the edgy punk rock style he idolized as a kid. Struggling with confidence, Patrick has hesitated to embrace his true self. As Clinton and Stacy guide him through this transformation, he rediscovers his identity and finally steps into the spotlight, fulfilling his teenage dream of ultimate cool.

programme.title: Powerhouse Diva
programme.desc: Clinton and Stacy’s new client, Freedom, is a two-time breast cancer survivor eager to reclaim her confidence. Seeking to embrace her inner diva, she needs help creating a style that reflects the resilient spirit she has lost. Clinton and Stacy help Freedom discover the power clothes can have on your life, giving the jumpstart she needs to enter a new chapter as a strong, empowered woman.

programme.title: Eclectic Bohemian
programme.desc: Aspiring artist Akemi has left a corporate job to pursue her dream as a visionary artist, but she struggles with personal style and craves her parents' approval. Enter Stacy and Clinton, ready to create a bold, artsy look that truly reflects her creative spirit. Together, they empower Akemi to embrace her vibrant identity, boosting her confidence to thrive as the artist she was meant to be.
